concentration.headline: Highly distributed urban network
concentration.body: No single city overwhelms Brazil's urban landscape. São Paulo holds 12.1% of tracked urban population across 2957 cities — characteristic of federal or historically polycentric countries with multiple strong economic centers.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United States, Germany, Switzerland

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — São Paulo & Guarulhos
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between São Paulo and Guarulhos (14 km apart, combined population 24,410,781). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 14 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: Brazil presents a classic polycentric structure: no primate city, with São Paulo holding just 12.1% of tracked urban population. Gravity is strongest within a tight sub-regional cluster (São Paulo → Guarulhos, 14 km average separation) rather than radiating from a single capital — typical of federal economies with distributed industrial heritage.
